title: Dishwasher deep-clean tab
subtitle: A compressed washing soda, citric acid, and salt tab for descaling and degreasing a dishwasher
excerpt: A monthly deep-clean tab keeps limescale from building up inside the dishwasher's spray arms, heating element, and pump. Washing soda cuts grease; citric acid dissolves mineral deposits. Pressed into an ice-cube tray and dried overnight, one tab runs through a hot cycle with no dishes inside. It is not a dishwasher detergent for everyday use.
sourceNotes: Dishwasher descaling tab. Washing soda (sodium carbonate) as primary degreaser; citric acid for limescale (calcium and magnesium carbonate) dissolution. Fine salt as abrasive filler and water softener. Castile soap as binder. Lemon essential oil for scent. Method: mix, press into moulds, dry 24 hours. Use one tab in detergent compartment or base of machine on empty hot cycle.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally for more tabs. Store finished tabs in a sealed container away from humidity.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 24 hours to dry fully before removing from the tray.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months in a sealed container away from moisture. Damp conditions trigger premature reaction between washing soda and citric acid.

glossaryTerms.definition: Sodium carbonate; a stronger alkali than bicarbonate of soda. Cuts through grease and food residues. Not the same as bicarbonate of soda (baking soda) and should not be used on aluminium surfaces.

Removing mineral deposits (primarily calcium carbonate) from water-contact surfaces such as heating elements, spray arms, and pump housings. Citric acid dissolves these deposits chemically without abrasion.

content.text: handles grease and general build-up. Citric acid handles

: the calcium and magnesium deposits from hard water that coat the heating element and block spray-arm holes over time. Run one tab through a hot empty cycle once a month. Do not use as everyday detergent; this formula has no surfactant for dish cleaning.

items.prepNote: Sodium carbonate; not bicarbonate of soda
Binder; drizzle carefully to avoid premature fizzing
Optional; add to soap before adding to dry mix

content.text: Combine all dry ingredients in the mixing bowl and stir thoroughly. If using lemon essential oil, mix it into the castile soap first. Drizzle the soap mixture over the dry ingredients very slowly while stirring. The mixture should hold together when pressed; it may fizz slightly as the soap touches the dry acids and alkalis. Work quickly.
Pack the mixture firmly into an ice-cube tray or silicone mould. Press down to compact each cube tightly. Leave uncovered at room temperature for 24 hours to harden. Store in a sealed container.
Place one tab in the detergent compartment or in the base of an empty dishwasher. Run on the hottest cycle available. Use monthly for maintenance in hard water areas, or every 2 months in soft water.
6 months in a sealed airtight container. Keep away from humidity. A tin with a rubber seal works well.
